context: External stabilization session for agents-lab 0.8 readiness. The factory is currently healthy: full local parity passed in the devcontainer, runtime health preflight returns continue, repo bloat/discourse/complexity gates are clean, and git is expected to be clean after this checkpoint. The default scheduler remains intentionally drained: only P3 parked/low-priority work should remain out of normal local-safe selection unless the operator explicitly chooses that lane. The remaining v0.8.0 readiness blocker is deliberate versioning only: packages are still 0.7.0, so target-version-ready stays false until the operator explicitly chooses the bump/tag/release lane. Recent work hardened runtime health, pi parity, cold capability handling for pi-lens, model-neutral colony policy, optional doctor auth checks, empty-queue scheduler guidance, provider-neutral distributed doctrine, local-stop intake guidance, seeded lane status guidance, and the /hatch/control-plane-continuity path so report-only read-only intent plans can run without redundant textual confirmation, shelling TUI slash commands, preparing workers before readiness, asking broad questions when the operator explicitly requests next local-safe seed material, or guessing inputs for dependent report-only route steps.
next_actions: Do not bump/tag/publish v0.8.0 without explicit operator release decision.
Before any long Pi runtime, run pnpm run pi:runtime:health or the equivalent environment_runtime_health_status gate in the current environment.
Keep the remaining planned external-influence tasks parked unless the operator explicitly selects a research lane with --priority p3 --include-protected or equivalent intent.

If continuing local-safe work while scheduler:next has no eligible task, route through operator_intent_intake_packet -> lane_brainstorm_packet -> lane_brainstorm_seed_preview in report-only mode before materializing any board task.
Prefer local-safe slices that improve packaging, docs contracts, engine boundaries, runtime pressure observability, first-party Pi stack distribution, or worker/intent contracts without dispatch.
blockers: target-version-ready: false by design until packages are intentionally bumped from 0.7.0 to 0.8.0
scheduler-default-queue: no eligible tasks because the remaining planned work is P3 parked/low-priority and intentionally excluded from default local-safe selection
